nltext:
(r)(ra) 2011-01-27 (Content Editors) OldMiner says You should be able to add some trailing text now as you add a firmlink. If a node already has a firmlink you want to add text to, remove it, then add it again with the text. Also, SIGTITLE should now be able to remove firmlinks (they couldn't before).
(r)(ra) 2011-02-12 (Content Editors) OldMiner says I just pushed out a couple of changes to productions. Let me know if you see bugs. (1) You should be able to delete VARS again, so removing your earphones if you go chatteroff will work. (2) Back to the old way of doing softlinks, with lastnode_ids on the links. By the magic of Javascript, the lastnode_ids will be invisible until you click the links. This should greatly reduce the random links between unrelated items in ENN that have been common with the cookie-based linking.
(r) 2011-02-14 dannye says re February 14, 2011: I knew a girl in college named Doreen. I fell in love with her name, if not more. I'm sorry for your loss and thinking about you.
(r) 2011-02-14 StuartO))) says re February 14, 2011: Your eulogy enriches us all. Thank you.
(r) 2011-02-14 androjen says re February 14, 2011: I'm so sorry to hear about your mother's passing. She sounds like she was a magnificent woman.
(r)(ra) 2011-02-16 (Content Editors) The Debutante says Hey People. Over the past two days, ALittleHawk has managed to be rude to three members of staff, sent offensive messages to Clockmaker, had a go at Halspal, and even accomplished the rather outlandish feat of irritating the anti-authoritarian Auduster. That's just the precis. I also happen to know that she has crossed swords with at least three other members of staff on earlier occasions. If anyone has any kind of positive relationship with her, could you please message me? Thanks.
(r)(ra) 2011-02-22 (Content Editors) The Debutante says Officers of the Good Ship E2. Captain Alex is currently enjoying a period of shore leave, and as a consequence the Royal Navy is in temporary command. All issues, whether technical, navigational, or personnel, should be addressed to Commodores Oolong, wertperch, or The Debutante. As you were.
(r) 2011-02-22 wertperch says Settings, too.
(r)(ra) 2011-03-03 (Content Editors) Oolong says Hey, it's been a while since anyone tried very hard to anthologise E2 much, so I've created an e2press group that gnarl's volunteered to lead. Please let me and/or him know if you're interested!
(r) 2011-03-18 Jet-Poop says I'm not sure I'd ever have the patience to make a movie, so good on ye. :)

(you have 462 private messages)

doctext: Removes a person's votes immediately and forbids them from voting inside of the vote opcode. They cannot vote, and those votes won't regenerate until the next day after this curse has been lifted.
title: vote

doctext: Removes a person's ability to C! something. Any unspent cools the user has are destroyed, and won't regenerate until the day after this hex has been repealed.
title: cool

doctext: Removes a person's ability to upload or change their homenode pic, and hides it from view.  This gives us the ability to take it away from people per our threat in that level power's documentation.
title: homenodepic
What to do if E2 goes down

doctext: User will be unable to use topic tokens to set the topic in the [E2 Gift Shop]
title: topic

doctext: Prevents publication of writeups by the suspended user.  User may still create drafts and request review.  Similar to [node forbiddance].
title: writeup

doctext: Prevents user from talking in any room.  Unlike borging, there is no place to put a descriptive explanation, and this does not automatically expire.  However, the suspended user will still be able to send private messages, which borging prevents.
title: chat

1: 
   2:   my $UID = $$USER{node_id};
   3: 
   4:   my $isGod = exists $HTMLVARS{'group_gods'}{$UID};
   5:   my $isEd = $isGod || exists $HTMLVARS{'group_content editors'}{$UID};
   6:   my $webloggables = getVars(getNode("webloggables", "setting"));
   7:   my $view_weblog ="";
   8:   # It might be more efficient to use a node_id for the VARS, but I'm not sure it'd make enough difference to be worth changing.
   9:   if ($$VARS{nodeletusergroup}) {
  10:     my $ug=getNode("$$VARS{nodeletusergroup}","usergroup");
  11:     $view_weblog=getId($ug);
  12:   }
  13:   else {
  14:     $view_weblog = 1328284; # default to E2science...
  15:   }
  16: 
  17:   # Most of the next chunk of code is pulled from [news archives].
  18: 
  19:   my @labels;
  20:   my $counter = 0;
  21:   my $limit = 14;
  22: 
  23:   my @groups = (); 
  24: 
  25: 
  26: 
  27:   foreach my $node_id (sort {
  28:         lc($$webloggables{$a}) cmp lc($$webloggables{$b})
  29:       } keys(%$webloggables)){
  30:     my $title =  $$webloggables{$node_id};
  31:     my $wclause = "weblog_id='$node_id' AND removedby_user=''";
  32:     my $count = $DB->sqlSelect('count(*)','weblog',"$wclause");
  33:     my $link = linkNode($NODE,$title,{'view_weblog'=>"$node_id"});
  34:     $link = "<b>$link</b>" if $node_id == $view_weblog;
  35:     push @labels, "$link<br /><font size='1'>($count node".
  36:     ($count==1?'':'s').')</font>';
  37:   }
  38: 
  39:   my $text;
  40: 
  41:   #$text.="nodeletusergroup: ".$$VARS{nodeletusergroup};
  42: 
  43:   return $text if (($view_weblog == 114)||($view_weblog==923653))&&(!($isEd));
  44: 
  45:   $text .= '<p align="center">'.
  46:            linkNode(getNode($view_weblog)).' writeups</p>';
  47: 
  48:   $text .= "<ul>";
  49:   my $wclause = "weblog_id='$view_weblog' AND removedby_user=''";
  50:   my $csr = $DB->sqlSelectMany('*','weblog',$wclause,'order by tstamp desc');
  51:   while(($counter <= $limit) && (my $ref = $csr->fetchrow_hashref())){
  52:     my $N = getNode($$ref{to_node});
  53:     next unless $N;
  54:     my $link = linkNode($N);
  55:     $text .= "<li>$link</li>\n";
  56:     $counter++;
  57:   }
  58:   $text .= "</ul>";
  59: 
  60: 
  61: # Pull a list of node_ids for groups the user can weblog to:
  62: 
  63: my $webloggables=$$VARS{can_weblog};
  64: my @groupids=split(',', $webloggables);
  65: 
  66: 
  67: # Put in a menu so the user can choose different groups:
  68: 
  69: $$VARS{nodeletusergroup} = 'E2science' unless $$VARS{nodeletusergroup};
  70: 
  71: my $str = htmlcode('openform');
  72: 
  73: $str.="\n\t<input type='hidden' name='op' value='changeusergroup' />";
  74: $str.="\n\t".$query->hidden('gochange','foo');
  75: $str.="\n\t<select name='group'>";
  76: 
  77: # get the titles of all the usergroups we need. I bet there's a more efficient way of doing this.
  78: for(@groupids) {
  79:  next if ($_ == 165580){ # Exclude 'News for Noders. Stuff that matters.'
  80:   push (@groups, getNodeById($_,"light")->{title});
  81:  }
  82: }
  83: 
  84: for(@groups) {
  85: 	$str.="\n\t\t<option value=\"$_\"";
  86: 	$str.=' selected="selected"' if $_ eq $$VARS{nodeletusergroup};
  87: 	$str.=">$_</option>";
  88: }
  89: $str.="\n\t</select>";
  90: $str.="\n\t".$query->submit("sexisgood","show");
  91: $str.="\n".$query->end_form;
  92: $text.=$str;
  93: 
  94:

doctext:
Do not impose this suspension. It is obsolete.

To prevent spam, new accounts used to be created with an "email suspension" which prevented them from chatting in any room or posting writeups, but not from sending messages. The suspension was lifted when they proved they had a valid email address. Since November 7, 2012 it has no longer been possible to log in to a new account at all without activating the account with a link sent by email, so this is no longer needed.
title: email
